content: Post-marketing safety signals trigger regulatory action when there is evidence of increased risk beyond the approved labeling. Causality assessment considers temporal relationship, biological plausibility, dechallenge/rechallenge patterns, and alternative explanations. Regulatory responses range from labeling updates (boxed warnings, contraindications) to restricted distribution, additional warnings, or market withdrawal in severe cases. Real-world evidence studies must address confounding through propensity score matching, instrumental variables, or other methods. FDA can require Phase IV trials as a condition of approval; failure to complete them can result in enforcement actions. Approximately 3-5% of approved drugs are eventually withdrawn for safety reasons.
